topic: Kubernetes requests and limits for production workloads
thread: Thread: Kubernetes requests and limits for production workloads for platform engineers. A short production view with the answer, architecture flow, and checklist. #Kubernetes #DevOps #CloudNative
Answer: Start from workload behavior, set requests from steady usage, set limits from failure boundaries, and validate with latency during scale events.
Architecture flow: Service -> Deployment -> requests/limits -> HPA -> PDB -> alerts -> rollback runbook
Production checklist: Check p95 latency, restart rate, throttling, pending pods, PDB coverage, and rollback path before calling a rollout production-ready.
My rule: if it cannot be reviewed, monitored, and rolled back, it is not production-ready yet. What would you add? #Kubernetes #DevOps #CloudNative

topic: Model registry approvals for production ML
thread: Thread: Model registry approvals for production ML for ML platform engineers. A short production view with the answer, architecture flow, and checklist. #MLOps #MachineLearning #AI
Answer: Treat every model like a controlled release: version data, code, metrics, approvals, artifact, deployment target, and rollback decision together.
Architecture flow: Data -> training pipeline -> MLflow run -> registry approval -> canary deploy -> monitoring -> rollback or promote
Production checklist: Track lineage, owner, metric threshold, canary window, drift signal, refresh trigger, and model retirement rule.
My rule: if it cannot be reviewed, monitored, and rolled back, it is not production-ready yet. What would you add? #MLOps #MachineLearning #AI
Thread: Terraform review checklist for cloud engineers. A short production view with the answer, architecture flow, and checklist. #Terraform #SRE #PlatformEngineering
Answer: Good infrastructure practice is repeatability plus reviewability. Plans, policies, approvals, state, and drift checks should be visible before apply.
Architecture flow: PR -> terraform fmt/validate -> plan -> policy check -> approval -> apply -> drift monitor -> GitOps sync
Production checklist: Review blast radius, state backend, IAM scope, module version, secrets handling, tags, cost estimate, and rollback notes.
My rule: if it cannot be reviewed, monitored, and rolled back, it is not production-ready yet. What would you add? #Terraform #SRE #PlatformEngineering
Thread: Data leakage in production ML for data and ML practitioners. A short production view with the answer, architecture flow, and checklist. #DataScience #MachineLearning #Analytics
Answer: Strong model work starts before training: define the business cost, validate data quality, prevent leakage, and choose metrics that match decisions.
Architecture flow: Question -> data contract -> split strategy -> baseline -> experiment -> validation -> monitoring
Production checklist: Check leakage, skew, missing values, imbalance, metric fit, explainability, deployment input contract, and feedback loop.
My rule: if it cannot be reviewed, monitored, and rolled back, it is not production-ready yet. What would you add? #DataScience #MachineLearning #Analytics
